title: Structural evolution of Ti/Cu multilayers as a function of period thickness
authors: Sergei S. Sakhonenkov, Aidar U. Gaisin, Anita O. Petrova, Vasilii A. Matveev, Elena O. Filatova
abstract: Ti/Cu multilayers with periods ranging from 4 to 52.5 nm were synthesized by magnetron sputtering to examine how the period thickness affects morphology, crystallization, texture development, and preservation of periodicity. The structural evolution was analyzed using complementary transmission electron microscopy techniques with X-ray diffraction and reflectometry. The results show that the period thickness governs the balance between interfacial transition-region formation, crystallization, and growth-induced morphological instabilities. At the smallest period of 4 nm, the structure has strongly reduced periodic contrast, which may be attributed to extended interfacial regions, partial Cu-Ti intermixing, and suppression of well-defined layer formation. At 10 nm, the multilayer structure is preserved but remains affected by accumulated roughness and layer waviness; local modification of the Cu lattice is suggested to be significant relative to the bilayer period, likely due to mixed Cu-Ti interfacial regions and/or coherent strain. With increasing period, the multilayers become more regular, and the layers show progressive crystallization and texture development during growth. Cu crystallizes predominantly in the fcc structure with a strong Cu(111) contribution, whereas Ti exhibits a more complex structural evolution, from an amorphous or weakly crystallized state near the substrate toward a more ordered and textured state closer to the surface.
summary: This paper examines how the thickness of Ti/Cu layers affects the growth and structural quality of multilayers. It finds that larger periods promote better crystallinity and regularity, while very thin layers suffer from interdiffusion and loss of periodic contrast.
main_problem: Investigating how the bilayer period thickness affects the structural evolution, morphology, crystallization, and texture development of Ti/Cu multilayers.
main_result: Increasing the period thickness leads to more regular structures with enhanced crystallization and texture, whereas very small periods (4 nm) exhibit significant intermixing and reduced periodic contrast.
method: Magnetron sputtering was used for synthesis, followed by characterization using TEM (STEM-HAADF, HRTEM), SAED, XRD, and X-ray reflectometry.
model_or_system: Ti/Cu multilayer coatings with periods ranging from 4 nm to 52.5 nm, deposited on Si(111) substrates via magnetron sputtering.
key_observables: Crystallinity, texture, layer roughness, layer waviness, interplanar spacings, and periodic contrast.
important_parameters: Period thickness (d) from 4 to 52.5 nm, layer thickness (l) from 1.00 to 26.25 nm, and total stack thickness of ~420 nm.
main_assumptions: The study uses periodic multilayers as model objects to separate basic mechanisms from the complexity of aperiodic supermirrors; XRD background was approximated by an exponential function.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows residual gas composition; Figure 2 shows STEM-HAADF cross-sections; Figure 3 and 5 show SAED and HRTEM for specific periods; Figure 8 shows XRD peak decomposition; Figure 9 shows XRR curves.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the scientific problem of structural evolution, describes the experimental synthesis and characterization setup, presents results for various period thicknesses, analyzes phase evolution and lattice expansion, and concludes with a discussion on growth-induced instabilities.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
